subtasks.description: Evaluate whether bulk PII export (names, emails, phone numbers) is permitted under data governance and privacy policies. Assess compliance with data classification, retention rules, and access controls.

plan.cost_rationale: This query explicitly asks for a bulk export of sensitive PII (names, emails, phone numbers). This is a governance and compliance decision, not a data extraction task. A policy_action subtask is required to evaluate the request against the policy registry before any SQL extraction is executed. Policy_action at mid tier is appropriate because PII export typically involves multiple applicable rules (data classification, retention policies, access control, privacy regulations) that require multi-signal reasoning. The request does NOT proceed to sql_gen until policy approval is obtained; the policy evaluation gates the entire operation. No sql_gen subtask is included in this plan because the query is asking for approval to export, not requesting the export itself post-approval."

output.reason: Bulk PII export by a data analyst lacks required Legal/CISO approvals (POL-501) and violates data minimization since no business-critical justification is provided (POL-502). Audit logging and retention controls (POL-503) can be satisfied conditionally if approvals are obtained.
applied_constraints: Provide business-critical justification or reduce scope to non-sensitive attributes
Enable audit logging with 30-day retention limit on exported data
